logs.name: closed-coda
logs.text: The name “Dear White People” is discriminating enough isn’t it? Imagine if it was “Dear Black People” with a bunch of white people on the cover. It’s a lot more negative now, and that’s what I don’t like. Can we not just agree to leave races out of it?

logs.name: corporate-mead
logs.text:
What is it specifically about *Dear White People* that you find aggravating?

The racism=prejudice+power formula comes from sociology. You should remember that this absolutely allows for black people to be racist against white people if they have power over those white people. But if we’re talking about America in the abstract, black people don’t have a lot of power, so you won’t see a lot of anti-white racism compared to ant-Black racism. Your not going to see white people incarcerated at highe

logs.name: cool-world
logs.text: @corporate-mead I get that the book title is meant to highlight the issue, but come on, you can't be serious about comparing incarceration rates. That's just plain old statistical data, not a reflection of racism! And @closed-coda, your point is valid. Why put race in the title at all if it's going to cause such a stir? Maybe they should just call it "Dear People" and let everyone decide their own race.

logs.name: timid-detail
logs.text: @corporate-mead I appreciate the sociological perspective, but sometimes I feel like the show is more about highlighting the internal struggles within the black community rather than just pointing fingers at white privilege. It's a complex issue, and I think the show does a good job of exploring that. @cool-world, while I agree that the title might be a bit provocative, I also think it's important to address these issues head-on. Maybe they could've handled it differently, but I don't think they deserved such harsh criticism.

logs.name: serious-delay
logs.text: @timid-detail I completely understand where you're coming from. While I do think the show could have been more nuanced, I also believe in holding media accountable for their messaging. It's important to push for better representation and dialogue, not just accept what we're given. We need to keep having these conversations to make real change. @corporate-mead, I agree that the title was provocative, but sometimes that's necessary to spark discussion. What do you think?

logs.name: moderator
logs.text:

The goal should be to foster meaningful discussions, not to alienate potential viewers. Plus, I think the show already does a good job of sparking those kinds of conversations."
